published.text:
### Hero
Heading: What to Automate First for Real Estate Teams
Body: If your real estate business knows it needs better automation, the first move usually should not be a giant all-in-one CRM rebuild. It should be one bounded workflow that fixes the leak already costing you conversations, showings, or follow-up discipline. For most agents and teams, that first project is one of four things: lead response while new inquiries are still deciding who answers first, showing coordination once a prospect wants the next step, CRM cleanup and pipeline automation when leads are getting lost between stages, or past-client reactivation when the database is full of people who already know you but never hear from you consistently. The right first build depends on where momentum is breaking today — before the first conversation, between inquiry and booked showing, inside the CRM itself, or after the deal closes when referral and repeat-opportunity follow-up quietly stops.
Sub: Below: how to choose the first real-estate workflow to automate, how this page stays distinct from the broader real-estate parent and the narrower lead-response and showing-coordination pages already live, what honest adjacent proof supports the recommendation, and when you should keep the first build smaller instead of forcing a broader ops overhaul too early.

### Core sections
- The first real-estate workflows usually worth automating
- Which real-estate workflow should you automate first?
- This page vs. the rest of the real-estate cluster
- Is this a good fit for your real-estate business?
- How to choose the first real-estate automation without overbuilding
- Relevant proof and adjacent proof

### FAQ
Subhead: Practical questions about choosing the first real-estate automation project
Topics: what a real-estate team should automate first, how this differs from the broader real-estate automation page, when lead response or showing coordination should come first, whether CRM cleanup should come before response automation, and when database reactivation is the better first build.

### Final CTA
Heading: Need help choosing the first real-estate workflow to automate?
Body: Book a 30-minute call. We will look at where your real-estate business is actually leaking lead momentum or team time today, tell you which workflow should come first, and show you what a smaller first build would need to include before you fund a broader automation rollout.

edits.description: Shipped from the roadmap's top queued candidate after broad backlog reconciliation still showed a clean surviving first-project gap in the real-estate cluster.

The live real-estate parent plus the lead-response and showing-coordination child pages already exposed distinct workflow families, but there was still no page helping an owner or team lead choose which bounded workflow should come first.

The page stays materially distinct from `/ai-automation-for-real-estate`, `/real-estate-lead-response-and-follow-up-automation`, and `/showing-coordination-automation-for-real-estate-teams` by staying only on the earlier process-selection decision itself.

Proof framing stays honest: the live real-estate cluster plus the published e-commerce CRM case study and adjacent response / booking proof support the workflow logic without pretending there is already a dedicated real-estate first-project case study.
